question: How do you configure Pebble log forwarding to Loki? Show the layer YAML and explain the protocol options.

claude_output.result:
## Pebble Log Forwarding to Loki

### Layer YAML

```yaml
log-targets:
  loki-prod:
    override: merge
    type: loki
    location: http://loki.example.com:3100/loki/api/v1/push
    services: [all]
    labels:
      app: my-app
      env: production
      owner: $OWNER   # interpolates from service env vars
```

**Key fields:**

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `override` | yes | `merge` or `replace` — how layers combine |
| `type` | yes | Protocol (see below) |
| `location` | yes | Remote endpoint URL |
| `services` | no | Defaults to nothing — omitting means no logs forwarded |
| `labels` | no | Added alongside auto-generated `pebble_service` label |

### Protocol Options

**`loki`** — Grafana Loki push API
```
http://<host>:3100/loki/api/v1/push
```

**`opentelemetry`** — OTLP/HTTP (logs signal)
```
http://<host>:4318
```
Note: no path suffix — Pebble appends the correct endpoint.

**`syslog`** — RFC 5424 syslog over TCP or UDP
```
tcp://<host>:<port>
udp://<host>:<port>
```

### Service Selection

```yaml
services: [all]          # all current and future services
services: [svc1, svc2]  # explicit list
services: [-svc1]        # exclude svc1 (useful when merging layers)
services: [-all]         # disable forwarding for this target
```

### Multiple Targets

You can define multiple targets simultaneously — useful for sending logs to different backends per environment:

```yaml
log-targets:
  loki-local:
    override: merge
    type: loki
    location: http://localhost:3100/loki/api/v1/push
    services: [debug-svc]
  loki-prod:
    override: merge
    type: loki
    location: http://loki.prod:3100/loki/api/v1/push
    services: [all]
    labels:
      env: production
```

The `pebble_service` label is always added automatically with the service name — you don't need to declare it.
